2603.17534.title: Informative Semi-Factuals for XAI: The Elaborated Explanations that People Prefer
2603.17534.paper_summary: Recently, in eXplainable AI (XAI), $\textit{even if}$ explanations -- so-called semi-factuals -- have emerged as a popular strategy that explains how a predicted outcome $\textit{can remain the same}$ even when certain input-features are altered. For example, in the commonly-used banking app scenario, a semi-factual explanation could inform customers about better options, other alternatives for their successful application, by saying "$\textit{Even if}$ you asked for double the loan amount, you would still be accepted". Most semi-factuals XAI algorithms focus on finding maximal value-changes to a single key-feature that do $\textit{not}$ alter the outcome (unlike counterfactual explanations that often find minimal value-changes to several features that alter the outcome). However, no current semi-factual method explains $\textit{why}$ these extreme value-changes do not alter outcomes; for example, a more informative semi-factual could tell the customer that it is their good credit score that allows them to borrow double their requested loan. In this work, we advance a new algorithm -- the $\textit{informative semi-factuals}$ (ISF) method -- that generates more elaborated explanations supplementing semi-factuals with information about additional $\textit{hidden features}$ that influence an automated decision. Experimental results on benchmark datasets show that this ISF method computes semi-factuals that are both informative and of high-quality on key metrics. Furthermore, a user study shows that people prefer these elaborated explanations over the simpler semi-factual explanations generated by current methods.
2603.17534.paper_summary_zh: 最近，在可解釋的人工智慧（XAI）領域，$\textit{即使}$解釋——所謂的半事實——已成為一種流行策略，解釋了預測結果如何在某些輸入特徵改變時仍然$\textit{保持不變}$。例如，在常用的銀行應用場景中，半事實解釋可以告訴客戶更好的選擇，即他們成功申請的其他替代方案，說 "$\textit{即使}$你要求的貸款金額是雙倍，你仍然會被接受"。大多數半事實 XAI 算法專注於尋找單一關鍵特徵的最大值變化，這些變化$\textit{不}$會改變結果（與通常找到最小值變化的反事實解釋不同，反事實解釋會改變結果的多個特徵）。然而，當前沒有任何半事實方法解釋$\textit{為什麼}$這些極端的值變化不會改變結果；例如，更具信息性的半事實可以告訴客戶，正是他們良好的信用評分使他們能夠借到雙倍於所要求的貸款。在這項工作中，我們推進了一種新算法——$\textit{信息豐富的半事實}$（ISF）方法——該方法生成更詳細的解釋，補充半事實，提供有關影響自動決策的額外$\textit{隱藏特徵}$的信息。基準數據集上的實驗結果顯示，這種 ISF 方法計算出的半事實在關鍵指標上既信息豐富又高質量。此外，用戶研究顯示，人們更喜歡這些詳細的解釋，而不是當前方法生成的簡單半事實解釋。
2603.17534.author: Saugat Aryal et.al.
